steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/ and sign in with your Google account
Create a new project or select an existing one
Navigate to APIs & Services > Library and enable the 'Google Docs API' and 'Google Drive API'
Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen
Fill in the app name, user support email, and developer contact email, then click 'Save and Continue'
On the Scopes screen, add the scopes: .../auth/documents and .../auth/drive.file, then click 'Save and Continue'
Add your own Google account as a test user, then click 'Save and Continue'
Go to APIs & Services > Credentials at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials
Select application type (e.g. 'Web application' or 'Desktop app')
Add authorized redirect URIs if applicable, then click 'Create'
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the dialog

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client ID
credential_fields.description: Your Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Client ID

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
credential_fields.description: Your Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 Client Secret

notes: Free tier: Google Docs API has no direct cost beyond Google Cloud project quotas
Requires OAuth consent screen setup before creating credentials
External apps stay in 'Testing' mode (100 users max) until verified by Google; verification required for production
Client Secret is shown at creation and can be re-downloaded from the Credentials page as a JSON file

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve a Google Doc by ID (requires a valid document ID and access token)
